response: Test variants allow you to create a single assessment that adapts to different candidate profiles. You can design multiple variants within the same assessment to personalize the evaluation based on roles, skills, or other criteria. This approach eliminates the need to manage separate tests, improves efficiency, and ensures candidates are assessed on the most relevant content. To create test variants, log in to your HackerRank for Work account, go to the Tests tab, open the test you want to modify, go to the Questions tab, click the more option menu (⋮) in the top right corner, and select Create Variants.
justification: The support ticket is asking about best practices for when to create a new test versus create a variant of the test. The provided support documentation includes a section on 'Create Test Variants' which explains the benefits of using variants and provides step-by-step instructions for creating them. [Source: data/hackerrank/screen/managing-tests/7530103378-test-variants.md] Ticket contains multiple requests; primary issue addressed, secondary requests may require follow-up.

response: To add extra time for a candidate, log in to your HackerRank for Work account and go to the Tests tab. Select the test you want to modify and go to the Candidates tab. Select the checkbox next to the candidate(s) and click More > Add Time. Enter the time in minutes and click Confirm. Please note that a candidate who is still in a test session must refresh the page to see the updated timer. A candidate who has completed the test must log in again to view the added time.
justification: The support documentation provides step-by-step instructions on how to add extra time for a candidate in a HackerRank assessment. [Source: data/hackerrank/screen/managing-tests/4811403281-adding-extra-time-for-candidates.md] Ticket contains multiple requests; primary issue addressed, secondary requests may require follow-up.

response: Unfortunately, once a conversation has been initiated, it cannot be deleted. However, your conversations are private and only accessible to a limited number of personnel involved in model training. If you have enabled Model Improvement in your Privacy Settings, your conversations may be used to improve Claude, but your incognito chats are not used for this purpose. You can adjust your privacy settings at any time.
justification: Based on the provided support documentation, there is no option to delete a conversation once it has been initiated.
